business_angle: Damo is the north-Leeds decking and fencing builder who gets it sitting true on the gardens that fight back - out-of-level ground, rock under the lawn, sloped driveways and retaining walls - and finishes it screwed and fixed, not nailed, dead straight.
why_this_trader_wins: Two independent reviews describe the same difficulty-handling specialism: Gail's decking went over uneven house levels, rock under the lawn and a raised drain chamber and still came out 'not a nail in sight, all screwed and fixed'; Lisa's fence ran dead straight across a sloped driveway and over a retaining wall. Combined with a whole-garden transformation delivered to an agreed timescale (the Boylans), this is precision outdoor construction on awkward ground, not generic 'garden services'.
target_customer: North-Leeds homeowners (Meanwood, Headingley, Chapel Allerton, Roundhay, Alwoodley and the LS postcodes) who want a deck or a fence built properly on a garden that isn't simple - uneven ground, slopes, retaining walls - and who want it to last.
page_single_job: Get that homeowner to request a quote (or call) for a decking, fencing or garden-transformation job.

customer_praise_themes.theme: builds true on difficult / uneven ground
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[1] (Gail): 'levels on the house were out - rock under the lawn ... a drain inspection chamber higher than I wanted the finished decking. Not a problem for Damo - he sorted it all out'; reviews[2] (Lisa): 'my driveway is sloped and also has a retaining wall beneath the fence'

customer_praise_themes.theme: screwed and fixed, not nailed (build method)
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[1] (Gail): 'constructed the decking to a high standard (not a nail in sight - all screwed and fixed)'

customer_praise_themes.theme: fences set out dead straight and strong
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[2] (Lisa): 'ensuring it goes in a straight line, and it's built to be as strong as can be'; reviews[3] (Alwoodley): 'professional fencing with a brilliant finish'

customer_praise_themes.theme: attention to detail / notices what others miss
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[2] (Lisa): 'He pays attention to detail, notices things I didn't see'; reviews[1] (Gail): 'His workmanship, attention to detail and efficiency are a credit to his business'

customer_praise_themes.theme: whole-area transformation delivered to an agreed timescale
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[0] (the Boylans): 'over the moon with the transformation of the area ... executed the plan to perfection, delivering everything within the agreed timescale'

customer_praise_themes.theme: quick to respond / personable and trustworthy
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[4] (Sarah): 'quick with correspondence'; reviews[2] (Lisa): 'I found him personable and trustworthy'

named_people.name: Damo
named_people.basis: business_name is 'Damo's decking and fencing' (brief.json business_name) and Damo is named in all 5 reviews. This overrides brief.json contact_name_usage_allowed=false, which concerns extracting an UNKNOWN possessive 'Damos' from one review's text, not the named proprietor in the business name itself. This is the Kyle Knowles ARCH-2 judgment-call precedent.

strongest_review_quote.text: Damo has just completed the most perfect decking. It wasn't easy - levels on the house were out - rock under the lawn (which explains all the moss!) and a drain inspection chamber higher than I wanted the finished decking. Not a problem for Damo - he sorted it all out - constructed the decking to a high standard (not a nail in sight - all screwed and fixed).
strongest_review_quote.author: Gail

real_services.name: Garden decking
real_services.evidence: reviews[1] Gail (raised decking over uneven levels, screwed and fixed); photos[] completed decking

real_services.name: Fencing
real_services.evidence: reviews[2] Lisa (fence, straight, strong, sloped driveway + retaining wall); reviews[3] Alwoodley (fencing, brilliant finish)

real_services.name: Groundwork and levelling
real_services.evidence: reviews[1] Gail (levels out, rock under lawn, drain chamber sorted before building)

real_services.name: Garden transformations
real_services.evidence: reviews[0] the Boylans (transformation of the area, plan executed to an agreed timescale)

claims_to_avoid: any certification or membership (Checkatrade Vetted, TrustATrader, Gas Safe, NICEIC) - none verified; directory_probes show 0 verified profiles (Checkatrade/Yell/MyBuilder/Trustpilot are snippet-only, exact-name-single-signal; TrustATrader was an identity homonym and rejected)

Google Places junk categories as services: brief.json services[] 'Building & construction' (Places category), 'Drain unblocking' (a MISREAD of Gail's 'drain inspection chamber' - Damo built decking around it, he does not unblock drains), 'landscapers' (niche label) are NOT real services
the street address '32 Sunset Hilltop' anywhere public - use Meanwood / Leeds LS6 only

service areas beyond north Leeds / LS postcodes - Roundhay (Gledhow) and Alwoodley are evidenced in reviews; the rest are UK-geographic derivations from the LS6 base and must stay plausible for a one-van trade (no national footprint)

drain unblocking, patios/paving, or block-paving driveways as services - not evidenced (the 'driveway' in reviews[2] is the customer's existing sloped driveway the fence runs along, not a driveway Damo laid)
personality: precise, grounded, dependable; a builder who sorts the hard ground first and finishes things properly rather than quickly
evidence_strength: moderate-content / single-source (rich, specific Google reviews + 9 completed-project photos; no third-party corroboration - Facebook/directory unverified)
pitch_hook_summary: Lead on the awkward-ground angle (decking true over rock/out-of-level houses, fences dead straight on slopes/retaining walls, all screwed not nailed), backed by 4.9/79 Google.

metadata.manual_review_note: Single-source (Google-only): no verified third-party profile and contactability=NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW (no email, UK mobile only). This is a BUILD run; pitch_gate enforces at outreach time, not build time. The evidence is specific and usable (same posture as the Kyle Knowles reference build, which was source_quality=FAIL). Source quality here is actually one notch better (PASS_WITH_WARNINGS).
metadata.authored_blind: Path B authored from frozen evidence + CLAUDE.md + the webfortrades-site-design skill only. Kyle's palette (wet-slate ink + viridian teal) and fonts (Bricolage Grotesque + Instrument Sans) are off-limits; this build uses an independent timber-and-iron palette + Oswald/Hanken Grotesk grounded in this trader's own materials.
